summary: Electric 1983 performance with blazing St. Stephen, Bertha and China Doll, but the transfer suffers from static on at least one track.
review: The show is remembered as a high‑energy night with standout moments like St. Stephen, Wang Dang Duh, Jack Straw, Birdsong, Bertha and a rare Phil‑led China Doll. Reviewers rave about the intensity of both sets, especially the pre‑drums run. The recording itself is disputed – one listener reports severe static on the St. Stephen track while another hears no problems, suggesting an uneven transfer.

ai_rating.rationale: Show quality is praised highly (mostly 5‑star comments) while recording quality has mixed feedback (one 1‑star complaint about static, one 4‑star approval), yielding a strong overall rating with moderate confidence.

recording_quality.technical_notes: One reviewer notes severe static on the St. Stephen track indicating a flawed transfer; another listener reports no audible problems, suggesting inconsistent source quality.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set packed with high‑energy highlights; second set builds even further with a rocking pre‑drums run and powerful renditions that many fans cite as unforgettable.

band_member_comments.Phil: Sang lead on China Doll (rare at the time) and used his own mic, which was noted as a special moment.
band_member_comments.Drums: Pre‑drums run described as "just rockin" indicating strong drum work.
